etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "value" + "-ed" from Latin "valere" + "-ed" (having been esteemed)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Having been esteemed; experiencing treasured appreciated and valued worth supported by appreciation awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Having been esteemed → treasured/appreciated → contemporary valued and being valued treasured and valued appreciated with developmental development
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Having been esteemed → treasured/appreciated → valued → understanding development

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Being valued treasured and valued appreciated; experiencing treasured appreciated and valued esteem calling for appreciation recognition
contemporary_usage.register: Positive relational; indicates worth calling for appreciation support and recognition development
